property.name: 17477a69-d9a5-4251-9329-0963e8121b5f
property.value:
### User Interface Modeling Workflow: 1. Understand the existing User Interface model. 2. Discover available Service operations (e.g. Commands, Queries, Service Operations, etc.) that are exposed over Http. 3. Design and create the Pages and Components needed to achieve the user's requirements 4. Connect these components to the necessary Service operations on the backend using the `Call Service Operation Action` associations. 5. Connect pages with `Navigation` associations together where there should be explicit navigations between them (e.g. buttons, links, clickable items, etc.) 6. Lay out all pages, navigations, and service calls on the diagram. Create the diagram if it does not yet exist, and then lay out the pages.
### User Interface Modeling Rules - Aways add `Navigations` between pages where there should be an explicit button, link or interface option that the user will be able to interact with to navigate to the target page. - Always express needed interactions with the backend using `Call Service Operation Action`. - Ensure that backend service operations are exposed over Http if they are located in a different application to this UI.
### User Interface Diagram Rules - Lay Pages out with space between one another, grouping by conceptual overlap and number of navigations. - Lay backend operations out to the right of the pages, vertically stacked and 100px apart (vertically). - If a `Navigation` between pages can go both ways, simply make the association bidirectional. DO NOT create two unidirectional associations. - Lay out Pages with separation in 2-dimensional space so that the associations don't stack on one another.
